items.caption_text:
Studies show that 75% Indians do not even have 6 months of emergency funds saved up, leaving them vulnerable to financial shocks. Only 1 in 4 Indians have a dedicated rainy day fund and roughly 45% of people save less than 10% of their income 

That we should consider the future value of money and not just what it means today was a big part of my discussion with @sandeepjethwani of @dezerv.in 

Full episode out now on @themoneymindset.co 🌟 

You can now find us on YT 📺 and Spotify 🎧 ✨

items.caption_text:
“The numbers feel overwhelming but the fact is If you are in the age group of 35- 40 and have expenses of 1-2 lakhs per month living in a metro in India today, you will need a corpus of 40 crores by the time you turn 60 years of age” says @sandeepjethwani of @dezerv.in 

We don’t realise that life expectancy has risen and with inflation and cost of living , we have to plan for retirement. 

Sandeep Jethwani is the founder of Dezerv , a wealth tech platform that manages an AUM of 16000cr . 

Sandeep has spent almost 20 years managing money for the wealthiest Indians, across geographies and businesses. 

Full episode drops tomorrow on @themoneymindset.co

items.caption_text:
HCL tech shares hit a fresh 52 week low this week and got hit hard post very weak earnings. 

With AI disruption, delay in decision making from large clients and deal cancellations , the sector is under pressure 

A few weeks ago I spoke to Kenneth Andrade of Old Bridge Capital on why he exited IT stocks completely earlier this year from his portfolio 

“We dont know whats happening in the tech sector. will we know in the next quarter ?  maybe, maybe not. 
If i had to build a portfolio today , I would build it without tech names” he says.

He explained that the terminal value of the IT businesses are falling and there is a lot of uncertainty in whether these businesses can continue to grow in the same way as earlier.

items.caption_text:
Spent a couple of days at the beautiful wellness centre @atmantan a few weeks ago. 

It was my first time at any wellness retreat. Thank you to the gracious hosts and founders for inviting me. @sharmilee_agrawal_kapur ❤️ 

Wellness has always felt like a privilege . You need time , access and most importantly, money. But in today’s fast paced , over stimulated world, it now feels like a necessity . 

The wellness industry in India is growing at a fast pace , it’s now a 160 bln $ industry in India and I was quite surprised to see how packed this place was. Everything from physical therapy, nutrition , spiritual healing , medically supervised diet programs it was all under one roof. 

A first time for me , the no sugar less salt diet was a bit tough 😅 but overall a great experience 💓

Having too many mutual funds in your portfolio is a common investing mistake we all make. Yesterday someone told me about having 40 funds in their folio 😅 . 

I’ve learnt over time ( and the hard way ) that 3-5 mutual funds are all you need for a strong well diversified portfolio. 

Thoughts 💭

items.caption_text:
24 hours in Delhi! ✈️ 

Sanjiv Bajaj and I spoke about the big trends shaping India today and the way forward . Sanjiv has built a full stack financial services company which includes insurance , lending and asset management. Bajaj Finserve is now a 6 lakh crore market cap company. He spoke to us about how to manage people, how brands are built to last for decades and how to adapt and stay relevant in today’s changing world. 

Thank you @eonewdelhi_official for inviting us ✨ 

Outfit @prinktonik 
Stylist : @shreemahajann

items.caption_text:
women often tell me that they are “not good with money” and struggle with basic financial decisions. One of the big reasons could be internalised patriarchy. 

Internalised patriarchy is real , it’s powerful , and often invisible. The belief that you are not capable of taking big financial decisions or handling money. 

women unconsciously adopt beliefs that limit their own financial power

The subconscious adoption of traditional gender roles that prioritize male financial control, devalue women's earning power, and perpetuate economic dependence. 

Unlearning years of social conditioning around money and finance is the first step to financial freedom as women 🎀

items.caption_text:
Lot of questions lately on international investing.

Here’s a starter pack. You should try and have 10-15% of your portfolio in international funds for diversification, hedging against the falling rupee and country specific risk. Also globally there are so many high growth areas like AI , deep tech, robotics , semi conductors that have large billion dollar companies. As an investor you should try and have exposure to future growth sectors for alpha generation. 

Nowadays there are so many ways to invest in international markets , many platforms that invest directly into the US markets, there is GIFT city route as well.

items.caption_text:
Here we go again.

US President Donald Trump threatened to block the Strait of Hormuz after negotiations between the US and Iran failed to yield any meaningful outcome over the weekend.

-Dow futures drop 500 points 
-Brent crude surges 7% to 102$/bbl

“Effective immediately, the United States Navy, the Finest in the World, will begin the process of BLOCKADING any and all Ships trying to enter, or leave, the Strait of Hormuz,” Trump wrote on Truth Social.

items.caption_text:
“We have gone out on a limb and told all our clients , this is the time to be topping up and not worrying about the situation ” Ravi Dharamshi of Value Quest tells me on The Money Mindset this week. @themoneymindset.co 

We did a deep dive into the macro events, how energy security is a big theme to watch and why he believes govt needs to do something drastic to get foreign flows back to move from vicious to virtuous cycle.

Ravi Dharamshi is the CIO of ValueQuest advisors, a SEBI registered PMS advisor. He has over 22 years of investing knowledge , is a deep thinker and student of the stock markets. Ravi spent his earlier years working and learning under Rakesh Jhunjhunwala at RARE enterprises before starting his own venture. 

Full chat out now ✨

items.caption_text:
Should you invest now or not? 

I know there is a ceasefire and markets have seen a big relief rally so the temptation is to invest now . But chasing headlines and being trigger happy can affect your portfolio in the long run. This is a volatile market and as we know anything can happen anytime.

So what do you do ? 

Whenever I think of putting fresh money to work I remember the wise words of veteran investor Sanjoy Bhattacharya who told me Sonia ask yourself three questions whenever you want to take a fresh investment call. 

1) what is your time horizon
2) what are your return expectations 
3) what is your propensity to bear losses in the near term ?

items.caption_text:
Multi asset funds are now emerging as the top favourite for investors. In February , record inflows were seen into multi asset funds. The assets under management of multi asset funds has doubled in the last one year. 

But why are investors piling into multi asset funds ? 
Because it is One fund investing into multiple assets - equity , gold and debt. And in a volatile market like this , the one thing you need in your portfolio is diversification.

There are many you can chose from
ICICI pru
SBI 
Nippon 
@groww_official all have great multi asset funds 

returns have been strong as well, ICICI pru for example has given 20% returns since inception of the fund , Nippon has given 22% over 3 years, kotak has given 20% and so on.
.
.
My t-shirt is from @lovenative.in 💙

items.caption_text:
Let’s understand what happened.

Oracle laid off 30,000 employees globally on Tuesday morning, roughly 18% of its global workforce, via a single email sent at 6am EST with no prior warning. 

System access was revoked almost immediately after. The cuts are expected to free up $8-10 billion in cash flow. 

Oracle’s stock has lost more than half its value since September 2025 and the company now carries over $124 billion in debt, up from $89 billion a year ago, with free cash flow running negative $10 billion last quarter.

What this means : 
Oracle posted a 95% jump in net income last quarter and still eliminated 18% of its workforce by email before most people finished their morning coffee. This is not a company in distress in the traditional sense. It’s a company that made an enormous debt-funded bet on AI infrastructure and is now converting its workforce into cash flow to service that debt.

raw.caption: 1st April, 2026: News just in : White House says Donald Trump to address the nation on Iran War at 6:30 AM IST on Thursday.

items.caption_text:
31st Mar, 2026: The Dow surges 1125 points overnight as optimism grows for an end to the Iran War . 

The Wall Street journal reported that Trump is willing to end military hostilities even if Strait of Hormuz remains largely shut. 

Trump indicates that Iran war could end soon. “We’ll be leaving very soon,” Trump told reporters at the White House.

The White House said that Trump will deliver an address “to the nation to provide an important update on Iran” at 9 p.m. ET Wednesday.

Unconfirmed reports now that Iranian president Masoud Pezeshkian was open to ending the war with guarantees. Pezeshkian had made similar remarks earlier this month as well.

items.caption_text:
As the famous saying goes , Markets can remain irrational longer than you and I can remain solvent. 😅

Am I buying this dip, yes I am. In hindsight , market corrections have been great buying opportunities and to me this is a once in a life time opportunity to buy quality stocks . 

Should you buy the dip ? Depends on your risk appetite , propensity for losses and income levels. Always take calculated risks and do what works for you. 

If you have the stomach for more losses, this may not be a bad time given that valuations are now at lowest level since covid.

items.caption_text:
March 27th 2026: Iran is slowly stripping away what seemed to be a US monopoly over economic warfare for decades. 

economic warfare has been a crucial plank of US power for decades - its ability to menace other countries economically, not least by sanctions. 
 
Iranian regime has not only survived this war for 26 days - it now seems to have greater negotiating power .

Thoughts ?
